service: Protect and advocate for consumers and ensure businesses comply with applicable laws and regulations
oneLiner: This work matters because before 1969 New Yorkers had no single city agency to turn to against fraud, mislabeled goods, or rigged scales, which is why the City Council created the country's first municipal consumer protection agency that year to police the marketplace.
summary: The Department of Consumer Affairs, DCWP's predecessor, was created on April 29, 1969 when the City Council passed the landmark Consumer Protection Law, merging the Department of Markets (including Weights and Measures) with the Department of Licensing into one unified agency. It was the country's first municipal consumer protection agency. Today DCWP continues that mandate by licensing businesses, inspecting scales and pricing practices, and enforcing rules like the ban on price gouging during emergencies, which can carry penalties up to $25,000 per violation.

sources.label: The Country's First Municipal Consumer Protection Agency Celebrates Its 50th Anniversary
